story_assessments.story_title: Search is becoming a start point for creator tasks
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, solo operators, and small-business owners
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: Starting a task in Search without retyping context into the next app
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google says AI Mode can connect apps like Instacart, Canva and YouTube Music directly to Search
story_assessments.stop_reason: Most people immediately understand the benefit of fewer copy-paste steps
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A cleaner search-to-app handoff for planning lists, visuals, or playlists
story_assessments.proof_point: Google Blog confirmed connected apps in AI Mode, with rollout starting in the U.S. this week
search_phrases: Canva in Google Search AI Mode
story_assessments.score_reason: Broad creator and small-business relevance, easy to explain quickly, and useful even to strangers who just want fewer steps between idea and execution.

story_assessments.story_title: Google Vids now covers more of the video handoff chain
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, video marketers, and small teams making short clips
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: Drafting and disclosing video content faster without jumping tools
story_assessments.interest_signal: Gemini Omni and personal avatars are now available in Google Vids for eligible users
story_assessments.stop_reason: The combination of faster drafting and SynthID disclosure is concrete and easy to visualize
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A one-workspace draft-to-edit workflow with built-in watermarks
story_assessments.proof_point: Google Blog confirmed Gemini Omni, personal avatars, and SynthID watermarks in Google Vids
story_assessments.score_reason: Very visual, practical, and easy to summarize in short-form video, with a clear workflow benefit and a built-in disclosure angle.

story_assessments.story_title: Jira is turning chat into structured work
story_assessments.target_audience: Product teams, software teams, and knowledge workers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: Reconstructing decisions and tasks after they move through chat, docs, and code
story_assessments.interest_signal: Jira Planner and Jira for Slack turn context from Jira, Confluence, Slack and GitHub into structured work
story_assessments.stop_reason: The conversation-to-ticket-to-review chain is easy to explain and relevant to many teams
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Keep the original thread alive as work moves across tools
story_assessments.proof_point: Atlassian says human review stays in the loop
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong operational value for teams that lose context between chat and tickets, with clear language and a recognizable workflow problem.

story_assessments.story_title: Zoom is giving sensitive workflows a different deployment path
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, client-facing teams, and internal ops handling sensitive data
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: Using AI when data must stay on customer infrastructure or private cloud
story_assessments.interest_signal: Zoom says AI On-Prem can run through Zoom Node on customer infrastructure or private cloud
story_assessments.stop_reason: Deployment model is a concrete differentiator that many people overlook
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A private AI path for summarization and search inside existing rules
story_assessments.proof_point: Zoom says it is available through Zoom Node add-on to paid Zoom Workplace plans
story_assessments.score_reason: Useful for sensitive workflows and governance-minded teams, with a clear deployment question even if it is less visually obvious than video.

tiktok.story_title: Search is becoming a start point for creator tasks
tiktok.target_audience: Creators, solo operators, and small-business owners
tiktok.opening: Would your search start the whole task?
tiktok.payoff: Google says AI Mode can connect apps like Instacart, Canva and YouTube Music directly to Search, so the first step can happen in one place and the next step can happen in the right app.
tiktok.selection_reason: Fast, relatable, and easy to explain in one breath. The handoff idea lands quickly for a broad creator audience and works well as a simple either/or prompt.

youtube.story_title: Google Vids now covers more of the video handoff chain
youtube.target_audience: Creators, video marketers, and small teams making short clips
youtube.payoff: Google says Gemini Omni and personal avatars are now available in Google Vids for eligible users, and generated clips include SynthID watermarks. That means a quicker draft-and-edit flow with disclosure built in.
youtube.selection_reason: Highly visual and easy to narrate for Shorts. The workflow and disclosure angle give viewers a concrete reason to keep watching and a clear question to answer.

facebook.story_title: Zoom is giving sensitive workflows a different deployment path
facebook.target_audience: Creators, client-facing teams, and internal ops handling sensitive data
facebook.opening: Can your meeting AI stay inside your environment?
facebook.payoff: Zoom says AI On-Prem can run through Zoom Node on customer infrastructure or private cloud for paid Zoom Workplace plans. For sensitive calls, internal training, or client material, the question is where the data lives as much as what the AI can do.
facebook.selection_reason: Facebook suits practical privacy and workflow discussions. This story invites experience-sharing from people who care about deployment rules and controlled environments.

pinterest.story_title: Google Vids now covers more of the video handoff chain
pinterest.target_audience: Creators, video marketers, and small teams making short clips
pinterest.opening: Google Vids Workflow for Faster Video Drafts
pinterest.payoff: Use Google Vids to draft and edit clips in one place, with Gemini Omni and personal avatars for eligible users and SynthID watermarks on generated clips. Save this if you make short updates, explainers, or training videos.
pinterest.selection_reason: This story has the strongest visual and searchable workflow angle for Pinterest, where practical guides and creator checklists perform well.

linkedin.story_title: Jira is turning chat into structured work
linkedin.target_audience: Product teams, software teams, and knowledge workers
linkedin.opening: Does chat keep losing your work context?
linkedin.payoff: Atlassian says Jira Planner and Jira for Slack turn context from Jira, Confluence, Slack and GitHub into structured work for agents, with human review still in the loop. The operational value is keeping the original thread attached as work moves into tickets and specs.
linkedin.selection_reason: Best fit for LinkedIn because it speaks to team process, operational consequences, and cross-tool handoffs in a professional setting.

overall_reasoning: The strongest pattern across the pack is workflow handoff, not isolated tool launches. I assigned each platform to the story that best matches its format: search-to-app for TikTok, video drafting and disclosure for Shorts and Pinterest, governed privacy for Facebook, and structured cross-tool work for LinkedIn. Every social asset stays within verified facts and opens with a direct, low-friction question.
